title: ZUCCHINI FRITTERS IN A PAN
instructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients. It is best to choose young zucchini with thin skin and small seeds.
2. First, they need to be washed well and dried thoroughly. Since I have young zucchini, I simply cut off the ends and cut out dark spots on the skin. If the zucchini is old, be sure to peel off the thick skin and remove the seeds. Grate the zucchini on a coarse grater. Squeeze the resulting mass well with your hands. If juice is released, drain it. I got some dry zucchini, there was no juice.
3. Add egg and salt to the zucchini, mix. Start adding flour in portions, stirring immediately to prevent flour lumps from forming. You may need a little less or more flour depending on the flour itself and the moisture of the zucchini. Heat a frying pan and pour oil into it. I have a non-stick frying pan, I just grease it with a small amount.
4. Spoon the batter in small portions. Do not make the fritters too thick, they may not cook through. Fry on one side until golden brown, then flip. Fry on the other side until done. If the fritters are hard to flip, add more flour to the batter. I advise frying a couple of fritters as a test to adjust the batter consistency.
5. Serve the zucchini fritters hot with sour cream or yogurt. Enjoy your meal!

title: SIMPLE FLUFFY APPLE CHARLOTTE IN THE OVEN
instructions:
1. Prepare the necessary ingredients. You can use one variety of apples or a mix. The main thing is that they are tasty, fragrant, and juicy. Use large, selected eggs. If the eggs are small, it's better to take one more. Preheat the oven to 180°C. Adjust the amount of sugar to taste.
2. Beat the eggs with a mixer until light foam. Then gradually add sugar (to taste) and continue beating until fluffy for about 3-5 minutes. To check if the eggs are beaten properly, run a spatula through the mixture. If the trail holds for 1-2 seconds, the eggs are beaten as needed. If the trail disappears instantly, continue beating. It's important not to overbeat. There should be small bubbles on the surface.
3. Flour needs to be sifted, as it becomes oxygenated and the dough becomes airy. Sift the flour through a sieve into a separate bowl with the baking powder – this will distribute it evenly in the dough. If you have time, it's better to sift twice.
4. Continuing to beat the eggs on low speed, gradually add the sifted flour to the dough. You may need less or more flour, watch the consistency. If you beat on high speed, the dough may deflate significantly and lose airiness. You can also use a spatula, but a mixer mixes the dough more effectively.
5. The dough should have the consistency of thick sour cream. However, air bubbles should remain in it, though not as many as before adding the flour.
6. Wash the apples, peel them, remove the core and cut into medium pieces. I didn't peel the skin because my apples have thin skin. You can take more or fewer apples to taste.
7. Add the apples to the dough and gently mix with a spatula. Line the baking pan (20-22 cm) with parchment paper and grease the bottom (but not the sides!) with butter. Pour the dough into the pan and smooth it out. You can evenly sprinkle a thin layer of sugar on top of the dough.
8. Bake the charlotte in a preheated oven at 180°C for about 40-50 minutes. Do not open the oven door for the first 25 minutes, otherwise the pie will sink. Check doneness with a wooden skewer – it should come out dry. If the top is already golden but the inside is still raw, cover the pan with foil and continue baking.
9. When the apple pie is ready, turn off the oven, slightly open the door and leave the pie inside for 10-15 minutes. This will help avoid a sharp temperature change, and the pie will not sink. Then remove the pan and let it cool completely on a wire rack. Serve the charlotte warm with tea, coffee or hot chocolate, sprinkled with powdered sugar. Enjoy your meal!

title: FLUFFY COTTAGE CHEESE PANCAKES IN A PAN
instructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients. Thoroughly wash the eggs under running water, combine eggs with sugar and whisk them with a whisk or fork. No need to beat until fluffy, just mix the ingredients together.
2. Use dry cottage cheese for the pancakes. If it is wet, the dough consistency will be runny and the pancakes may not turn out well. The consistency of the cottage cheese should be fine-grained. To achieve this, you can rub the cottage cheese through a sieve.
3. Add the cottage cheese to the bowl with the eggs and mix everything. I do not recommend using a mixer for curd dough, as beating always makes the curd dough runny. Using a wooden spatula or a simple whisk is sufficient.
4. Then add sifted flour, soda, and vanillin to the dough. Do not use a mixer for mixing. You may need a little more or less flour, guide yourself by the consistency of the dough.
5. Using a spoon moistened with water, separate a small amount of curd dough and roll it in flour. Form a small pancake with your hands. Do not ignore this step. When rolled, the outer part of the pancake becomes thicker and, when fried, creates a crispy crust. The inside of the pancakes will remain soft and tender.
6. Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan. Fry the pancakes over medium heat for about 1-2 minutes per side. Depending on the stove and the thickness of the pancakes, the cooking time may vary. In this case, you can additionally send them to bake in a preheated oven for about 10 minutes at 180 degrees.
7. Complement the pancakes with your favorite jam, sour cream or condensed milk. Serve for breakfast, afternoon snack or snack together with a cup of aromatic tea, coffee or cocoa. Enjoy your meal!

title: CLASSIC COTTAGE CHEESE PANCAKES IN A PAN
instructions:
1. Prepare the products. Take cottage cheese without milk fat substitute, not runny, quite dense. In my opinion, pancakes made from fattier cottage cheese (9-18%) are tastier. Frying the patties will take about 100-150 ml of oil, the amount indicated in the ingredients (20 ml) is the oil that will be absorbed into the pancakes and will be taken into account when counting calories.
2. Mash the cottage cheese with a fork. Look at the consistency of the cottage cheese: if it has grains, it is best to rub it through a fine sieve or use an immersion blender, then the texture of the cottage cheese will become more uniform.
3. Beat the egg into the cottage cheese. Before using, wash the egg with a cleaning agent in running water, as there may be harmful bacteria on its surface. Mix everything well with a fork. Add salt and mix again. Add sugar. I use regular white sugar, but you can also cook with brown sugar, it will give the pancakes a caramel flavor. Also at this stage you can add vanilla or vanilla sugar, as well as cinnamon to the dough.
4. First, sift the flour. I recommend not neglecting this process, as during sifting the flour is saturated with oxygen, and the finished pancakes will turn out more airy and tender. Add the flour to the cottage cheese and mix everything well. Depending on the quality of the flour and the moisture of the cottage cheese, you may need a little more or a little less.
5. Take some dough and form a small patty. I poured flour for breading into a plate by eye - for convenience. About 1 tablespoon of flour will remain on all the pancakes. Roll the future pancake on all sides. Use all the dough this way.
6. Heat the oil well in a frying pan. I needed 100 ml. Place the pancakes and cook over low heat for about 5-7 minutes. Carefully flip the pancakes and cook for another 6-8 minutes over the lowest heat. You can cover the pan with a lid.
7. Serve the fluffy pancakes hot or warm. Classic additions are sour cream, condensed milk, berry jam or fresh berries. Greek yogurt and honey are great for a healthy breakfast. Children will appreciate serving with fruit syrup or chocolate sauce. Enjoy your meal!

title: FLUFFY COTTAGE CHEESE CASSEROLE IN THE OVEN
instructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients. Take cottage cheese that is fattier, at least 5%. Then the casserole will be really tasty, not dry. Also choose sour cream that is fattier. If you have thick sour cream, put less of it, if runny - more. You can change the amount of sugar to your taste. To allow the oven to heat up to 180°C, turn it on in advance, about 10-30 minutes before starting cooking.
2. Take a bowl, put the cottage cheese in it. In this recipe, all ingredients are blended with an immersion blender. If you don't have one, rub the cottage cheese through a sieve - it will become homogeneous. Add sugar. Add baking soda. It is necessary for the fluffiness of the casserole. And salt to enhance the taste. Add vanillin for aroma.
3. Thoroughly wash the eggs under running water. Take large, selected eggs. If the eggs are smaller, increase their quantity. If you don't have a blender, beat the eggs with sugar separately, and only then add to the main mixture. If you have a blender, immediately break the eggs into the cottage cheese. Add sour cream. Consider what kind of cottage cheese you have - dry or wet - and adjust the amount of sour cream.
4. Blend all ingredients with an immersion blender - the mass will become homogeneous and creamy. Add semolina. Its amount can also vary. If the mass was initially thick - put less, if too runny - put one more spoon. Consider that it will still swell during baking. Do not overdo it. Mix the mass well. Leave it to stand for 20 minutes, during which time the semolina will swell.
5. Prepare the baking dish. Grease it with a piece of soft butter, then sprinkle with semolina. The semolina will stick to the butter and become a crust during baking. This will help easily remove the casserole from the mold.
6. Pour the batter into the mold, smooth the top with a spatula so that the casserole rises evenly during baking. My mold size is 20*20 cm. If you use a smaller mold, the casserole will be taller.
7. Bake the casserole in a preheated oven at 180°C, top-bottom mode, for about 30-40 minutes, until golden on top. The time is approximate. How to check if the casserole is ready? As soon as a light golden crust forms on the surface, take a skewer and pierce the casserole in several places. If the skewer is dry, then the casserole is ready. Cool the finished casserole completely and only then remove from the mold.

title: COTTAGE CHEESE COOKIES IN THE OVEN
instructions:
1. Prepare the necessary ingredients. Use high-grade flour. Use moderately moist cottage cheese, not lumpy, without small hard grains, 5-9% fat. Cottage cheese in briquettes is perfect. If the cottage cheese is lumpy and dense, it is better to rub it through a sieve or blend it with a blender. Melt the butter in a convenient way and cool to room temperature.
2. In a small bowl, mix sugar and cinnamon and set aside. This mixture is not added to the dough, but is needed to dip the preparations into.
3. In a bowl, combine cottage cheese, melted butter, vanilla sugar and soda. Mix everything thoroughly until smooth. You can beat the mass with a mixer, or you can simply mash it with a fork.
4. Gradually add the sifted flour. You may need more or less flour. So do not rush to add all the flour at once. Look at the consistency. The dough itself will take as much flour as it needs. Knead a soft, elastic, non-sticky dough, adding a little more flour if necessary. Roll the finished curd dough into a ball, wrap in cling film and refrigerate for 40 minutes.
5. Roll out the chilled dough on a floured table into a layer about 5 mm thick. Use a glass or cookie cutter to cut circles (Ø 8-10 cm) from the dough. Gather the remaining dough between the pieces into a ball, roll out and cut out the pieces again. Preheat the oven to 190°C in advance.
6. Place a dough circle on the sugar and press with your fingers. Then fold the dough in half with the sugar side inward. Dip one half into the sugar again and fold in half with the sugar inward. Roll the resulting quarter on one side in the sugar. The other should remain clean. Transfer the pieces to a baking sheet lined with parchment paper, sugar side up.
7. Bake the cookies in a preheated oven at 190°C for about 15 minutes, until golden brown. The exact time depends on your oven and personal preference. Make sure the cookies do not burn on the bottom.
8. Serve homemade cottage cheese cookies cooled with hot tea, coffee, a glass of milk or cocoa. You can complement with berry sauce, whipped cream or a scoop of ice cream for a more dessert-like option. Enjoy your meal!

title: POTATO PANCAKES (DRANIKI) IN A PAN
instructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients. Wash the potatoes under running water using a brush or stiff sponge, peel and grate on a medium grater. If desired, the potatoes can be squeezed from the juice, washed from excess starch, then the pancakes will turn out snow-white. But it's better not to do this, as the pancakes will be tastier, crispier and browner.
2. Put the grated potatoes in a deep bowl. Add salt and ground black pepper to taste. Beat in the chicken egg. Wash the egg in advance under running water. Mix the mass until smooth.
3. Add flour to the potato mixture. Add it spoonful by spoonful, mixing well each time to track the consistency of the dough. The dough should be quite thick, thicker than sour cream. Then the pancakes will turn out plump and fluffy. Depending on the quality of the flour, you may need a little more or a little less.
4. Once the dough is ready, pour vegetable oil into a frying pan and heat it. The amount of oil is approximate and depends on the volume of the pan.
5. Heat the vegetable oil in the frying pan. Spoon the batter onto the pan, forming neat round pancakes. Fry without a lid for 3-4 minutes until a golden brown crust appears on the bottom. Then carefully flip and fry for another 2-3 minutes on the other side. If the pancakes turn out thick, cover the pan with a lid for 1-2 minutes at the very end of frying. The time is approximate, judge by the color of your pancakes.
6. Carefully remove them with a wooden spatula onto a plate lined with a napkin so that the remaining vegetable oil is absorbed into it. Serve the potato pancakes with sour cream and fresh vegetables and herbs. Enjoy your meal!

title: FLUFFY PANCAKES WITH MILK
instructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients. Use fresh milk and eggs, olive oil - refined (extra virgin olive oil will not work, as it will give bitterness). Olive oil can be replaced with any other refined oil without a strong taste or smell.
2. In a deep bowl, mix all the dry ingredients: flour, soda, citric acid, sugar and salt. Citric acid in the dough works in tandem with soda. If you don't have citric acid, you can add about 1-1.5 tsp of lemon juice, but this should be done in step 3, where the liquid ingredients are added. Take a whisk and mix the dry ingredients for about 30 seconds. This is necessary for the correct and even distribution of all components.
3. Add the egg to the flour mixture, mix with a mixer, then the milk, mix again. Add 3 tablespoons of olive oil and finally mix everything for about two minutes.
4. You should end up with a fairly thick dough (if you turn a spoon over, it will rather fall in lumps than drip off it). In fact, after the first time you will immediately know the correct consistency of the dough. If necessary, add a little milk or flour.
5. Heat a frying pan over medium heat WITHOUT OIL. Spoon the pancake batter onto it. When is it time to flip the pancakes? Wait for bubbles to appear all over the surface of the pancake. Then you can flip.
6. Usually, frying takes 2-3 minutes per side. However, keep in mind that the exact time will depend on the size of the pancakes, the chosen pan, and the features of your stove.
7. Serve the fluffy milk pancakes in a stack, generously drizzled with maple syrup or honey. The classic serving is with a pat of butter melting on the hot surface. For contrast of tastes, add berries, banana slices and a sprig of mint. Enjoy your meal!

title: CLASSIC HOMEMADE PIZZA WITH SAUSAGE IN THE OVEN
instructions:
1. Start making the pizza with the dough. Prepare all the necessary ingredients. Olive oil is used in the classic pizza dough recipe. But it can be replaced with any other refined vegetable oil. Use high-grade flour. It must be sifted to remove fine debris and saturate with oxygen, then the dough will rise well and be airy.
2. Add sugar and yeast to warm water (37-40°C). Stir with a spoon until the sugar is completely dissolved. In the absence of a special culinary thermometer, the required temperature can be determined as follows: you need to drop a drop of water on the inside of your wrist. If the sensation is neutral - neither cold nor hot - then the temperature is correct.
3. If the temperature is too low, the yeast will work slower and more time will be needed for preparation. At elevated temperatures, the yeast will die and the dough will not rise. Add 2 tablespoons of flour, stir and leave for 10 minutes.
4. If a fluffy cap appears on the surface, the yeast has been activated and you can continue cooking. If this did not happen, then the yeast will have to be replaced. The dough will not work with such yeast.
5. For kneading the dough, take a deep bowl in which it will be convenient to mix dry and liquid ingredients. Pour half of the flour into it. Add salt and mix it with the flour so that the dough is evenly salted. Make a well in the center of the flour.
6. Pour the vegetable oil and the dissolved yeast into the flour. Add the liquid component to the dry one, not vice versa. This way the dough will be most successful. Since the properties of flour of the same grade but from different manufacturers may differ, to achieve the desired consistency, add the flour in small portions. When it becomes difficult to mix with a spoon, start doing it with your hands. Knead the dough thoroughly.
7. It should turn out soft and tender, but not stick to your hands. Grease the bottom and sides of the bowl with vegetable oil to prevent the dough from sticking to the dish. Also knead the finished dough with oiled hands. Cover the bowl with a napkin or towel to prevent it from drying out and rising well. Leave in a warm place for about 1-1.5 hours. During this time, the dough should increase several times. About half an hour before starting cooking, turn on the oven to 200 degrees.
8. Choose any cheese that melts well. Take the sausage you like best. Red onion is tastier - it has not such a sharp onion aroma and a sweeter taste.
9. Cut the sausage into strips - this is the standard cut for homemade pizza. Grate the cheese on a coarse grater.
10. Wash the tomato, dry it, remove the stem and cut into thin slices. Cut the olives into rings - it is better to remove the tails, with them the pizza will not look very neat.
11. Peel, wash and cut the onion into half rings. Heat a frying pan with oil. Fry the onion in it for 3 minutes, stirring constantly. It should not become too soft, but just slightly wilted. Cool completely.
12. Knead the dough with your hands into a round layer directly on parchment paper. Leave the edges thicker to form a rim. From this amount of dough you can make one large pizza or two small ones. I will make two pizzas with a diameter of about 20 cm each.
13. Evenly distribute half of the cheese, then evenly distribute the onion. Next layer the sliced sausage, then the tomato. Sprinkle all with the remaining cheese and evenly distribute the olives.
14. Bake for 15-20 minutes on the middle rack at 200 degrees in top-bottom mode. The cooking time depends on the characteristics of your oven, so check for doneness. In the finished pizza, the dough will rise and slightly brown. When gently pressed with the palm, the dough should not "wobble".
15. Serve homemade pizza hot, straight from the oven, cut into triangles. Garnish with fresh herbs (basil, arugula). Pairs well with tomato or garlic sauce. The ideal drink is cold lemonade, beer or light wine. You can take this classic sausage pizza with you on a picnic. Enjoy your meal!

title: FLUFFY OMELET WITH MILK IN A PAN
instructions:
1. Prepare all the necessary products for the perfect omelet. Take larger eggs. Choose natural milk, the percentage of fat does not matter. Vegetable milk will also work. For ideal fluffiness and even baking, it is better to use eggs and milk at room temperature.
2. Wash the eggs well but carefully. There can be a lot of bacteria on the eggshell. Carefully crack the eggs into a deep bowl. Beat everything with a fork so that the yolk and white are well mixed.
3. Pour in the milk. How much milk per egg for an omelet? It is traditionally believed that for one egg, take the amount of milk that fits in half of that egg's shell. So you can also use the eggshell to measure the amount of milk. Mix the mass well with a fork.
4. Salt the omelet to your taste. I use regular sea salt, but salt with various additives will also work. At this stage, you can add various goodies to the omelet mixture that you like. Fresh and dried herbs, onions, garlic are appropriate here. Or perhaps you prefer a heartier dish, then add pieces of bacon, chicken, grated cheese and pieces of tomato, sausage, frankfurters. I am preparing a classic version of the omelet, so I have no additions.
5. I use cast iron cookware, but it can also be a frying pan with ceramic or simple non-stick coating. Grease the pan with oil. It can be any vegetable oil, but always refined, so as not to burn in the pan. After that, heat the pan well over medium heat.
6. Pour the omelet mixture into the pan. Be sure to cook over low heat, as the omelet burns very quickly. The cooking time is about 8-10 minutes. The exact time depends on the features of your stove and the chosen pan. To ensure the omelet cooks well on top and is fluffy, cover it with a lid. After 5-6 minutes, check the omelet for doneness.
7. Remove the finished omelet from the heat and leave under the lid for another 5 minutes. This way the omelet will not settle and will reach full readiness.
8. Serve the best omelet immediately hot, cut into triangles or squares. Garnish with fresh herbs (dill, parsley), cherry tomato slices or avocado slices. A few slices of crispy toast or a light vegetable salad will ideally complement the picture. It is best to eat freshly prepared fluffy omelet immediately. Store the cooled dish in the refrigerator in a sealed container for up to 24 hours. Enjoy your meal!

title: KEFIR OMELET IN A PAN
instructions:
1. How to make an egg and kefir omelet in a pan? Very simple and easy! First, prepare the necessary ingredients according to the list. It is better to use kefir and eggs at room temperature, because salt does not dissolve well in a cold mixture. Instead of vegetable oil, you can use butter to grease the pan.
2. Wash the parsley, dry it and finely chop it. If desired, you can replace or supplement with other herbs, for example, dill, cilantro, green onions or use a mixture of different herbs.
3. Grate the cheese on a coarse or medium grater. Any cheese will work for this dish - hard, semi-hard, soft, like mozzarella. The main thing is that it is tasty, high-quality, without milk fat substitutes and melts well.
4. Crack the eggs into a deep bowl. Add salt and pepper. If the eggs are small, take 3 pieces.
5. Pour in kefir at room temperature. Beat everything with a whisk until smooth. No need to beat for a long time. The salt crystals should completely dissolve.
6. Sift the flour through a sieve into the omelet mixture and stir, breaking up any lumps. The mixture should have the consistency of liquid sour cream. You can make it thinner if you prefer a more tender omelet.
7. Add chopped herbs to the omelet and stir.
8. Heat vegetable oil in a frying pan over medium heat. Pour the egg-milk mixture into the pan. Sprinkle grated cheese on top.
9. Reduce heat to low and fry the omelet under the lid for 5-7 minutes. Consider that the diameter and thickness of the finished omelet directly depend on the size of your pan. My pan has a diameter of 22 cm.
10. Transfer the finished omelet to a plate and serve. Enjoy your meal!

title: FLUFFY OMELET WITH MILK AND SAUSAGE IN A PAN
instructions:
1. How to make an omelet with sausage? Prepare the products. Initially, the recipe is for 4 servings, so the description uses 6 eggs. I cooked from two. It will be tastier to take cooked sausage, without fat. Frankfurters will also work. You can skip the vegetables altogether, they are for those who like them and for health and beauty. You can also add any greens - dill, parsley, green onions. Wash and dry the vegetables in advance.
2. Cut the sausage into small cubes. The smaller they are in size, the more aesthetically pleasing the omelet will look, but in general, it's not critical.
3. Also cut the tomato into cubes.
4. And the bell pepper. If you take peppers of different colors, it will turn out even more beautiful.
5. Crack the eggs into a bowl and beat with a whisk until fluffy.
6. Pour in the milk, add salt and pepper. Consider the saltiness of the sausage, do not over-salt the omelet. Beat all the products with a whisk again until completely smooth.
7. Add the chopped sausage and vegetables to the egg mixture. Mix well again.
8. Take a suitable frying pan. The smaller its diameter, the fluffier the omelet will be. Heat it over low heat, pour in vegetable oil. Let the oil also warm up a bit. Pour the egg mixture into the pan.
9. Cook the omelet for about 5 minutes over low heat, then cover the pan with a lid and turn the heat to low.
10. After 10 minutes, the fluffy omelet will be ready. The eggs should be fully cooked - if they remain runny on top, keep the omelet on the heat a little longer. Serve the omelet immediately - it tastes better hot, besides, such an omelet quickly deflates like a souffle. When serving, sprinkle the omelet with herbs.

title: OMELET WITHOUT MILK IN A PAN
instructions:
1. How to fry an omelet without milk in a pan? Prepare all the necessary products. For making an omelet, take larger eggs, preferably homemade.
2. Wash the egg well and be sure to check for freshness. Fresh eggs are heavier in weight, while old ones are light, as if dried out inside. To check, put the egg in water at room temperature: a fresh egg will sink, an old one will float to the surface. Crack the eggs into a small bowl. Take a deeper bowl so that the egg mixture does not spill out of it.
3. Beat the eggs with a fork or whisk, not a mixer. You need to beat the eggs slightly.
4. Salt the egg mixture. Also at this stage, you can add some spices to the egg mixture. It could be dried garlic, dill and other herbs. Or maybe some herbs: thyme, rosemary, tarragon! Rely on your taste!
5. Add butter to the pan for making the omelet. It is advisable to use a pan with a non-stick coating.
6. Completely melt the butter.
7. Pour the melted butter into the egg mixture, stirring constantly.
8. Pour the egg mixture into the same pan where the butter was melted. Cook over low heat on one side only, trying to ensure the omelet is fully cooked.
9. Roll the omelet into a roll, fry again on all sides. If necessary, bring to full readiness under the lid.
10. Place the omelet on a plate and serve hot. It is great to serve such an omelet for breakfast or afternoon snack for both adults and children. Quick and easy to prepare and always turns out well. Enjoy your meal!

title: CHERRY JELLY
instructions:
1. Prepare the ingredients. Wash and dry the cherries. Defrost frozen berries, save the juice.
2. Pour gelatin into a glass. Pour 1/7 part of cold water. Leave for about 20 minutes to swell. Follow the package instructions.
3. Divide the berries into two parts. Remove pits from one part of the berries.
4. Put the berries with pits into a saucepan, pour in the remaining water, add sugar and stir.
5. Stirring occasionally, bring the water to a boil. Cook the compote over low heat for about 5 minutes. Remove from heat and let steep for about 10 minutes.
6. Strain the finished compote through a sieve, squeeze the berries.
7. Add the swollen gelatin to the hot compote. Mix well until completely dissolved.
8. Place the pitted cherries on the bottom of the ramekins.
9. Pour the jelly mixture over the cherries.
10. Put the jelly in the refrigerator until completely set. Garnish with fresh mint before serving. Enjoy your meal!

title: SIMPLE FLUFFY APPLE CHARLOTTE IN THE OVEN
instructions:
1. Prepare the necessary ingredients. You can use one variety of apples or a mix. The main thing is that they are tasty, fragrant, and juicy. Use large, selected eggs. If the eggs are small, it's better to take one more. Preheat the oven to 180°C. Adjust the amount of sugar to taste.
2. Beat the eggs with a mixer until light foam. Then gradually add sugar (to taste) and continue beating until fluffy for about 3-5 minutes. To check if the eggs are beaten properly, run a spatula through the mixture. If the trail holds for 1-2 seconds, the eggs are beaten as needed. If the trail disappears instantly, continue beating. It's important not to overbeat. There should be small bubbles on the surface.
3. Flour needs to be sifted, as it becomes oxygenated and the dough becomes airy. Sift the flour through a sieve into a separate bowl with the baking powder – this will distribute it evenly in the dough. If you have time, it's better to sift twice.
4. Continuing to beat the eggs on low speed, gradually add the sifted flour to the dough. You may need less or more flour, watch the consistency. If you beat on high speed, the dough may deflate significantly and lose airiness. You can also use a spatula, but a mixer mixes the dough more effectively.
5. The dough should have the consistency of thick sour cream. However, air bubbles should remain in it, though not as many as before adding the flour.
6. Wash the apples, peel them, remove the core and cut into medium pieces. I didn't peel the skin because my apples have thin skin. You can take more or fewer apples to taste.
7. Add the apples to the dough and gently mix with a spatula. Line the baking pan (20-22 cm) with parchment paper and grease the bottom (but not the sides!) with butter. Pour the dough into the pan and smooth it out. You can evenly sprinkle a thin layer of sugar on top of the dough.
8. Bake the charlotte in a preheated oven at 180°C for about 40-50 minutes. Do not open the oven door for the first 25 minutes, otherwise the pie will sink. Check doneness with a wooden skewer – it should come out dry. If the top is already golden but the inside is still raw, cover the pan with foil and continue baking.
9. When the apple pie is ready, turn off the oven, slightly open the door and leave the pie inside for 10-15 minutes. This will help avoid a sharp temperature change, and the pie will not sink. Then remove the pan and let it cool completely on a wire rack. Serve the charlotte warm with tea, coffee or hot chocolate, sprinkled with powdered sugar. Enjoy your meal!

title: SIMPLE CHERRY PIE IN THE OVEN
instructions:
1. How to bake a simple cherry shortbread pie in the oven? First, prepare the necessary ingredients according to the list. Start cooking with the filling. It is better to make the filling in advance, for example, in the evening. It will stabilize and thicken overnight.
2. In a small bowl, mix sugar, starch, cinnamon and vanillin. This will prevent lumps.
3. You can use fresh or frozen cherries. Defrost frozen cherries in advance, place them together with the juice in a saucepan. Add sugar with starch and gently mix.
4. Bring the mixture to a boil over medium heat and cook, stirring, for about 1 minute until thickened.
5. Cool the filling completely, covering with plastic wrap.
6. Now prepare the shortcrust pastry. Use high-grade flour. The butter, milk and eggs should be cold, so take them out of the refrigerator immediately before cooking.
7. In a bowl, combine cold milk, sugar, vanilla sugar, egg, yolk and salt. Mix with a whisk until smooth.
8. In a blender bowl, mix flour, cubed cold butter and baking powder. Blend everything into fine crumbs. Combine the crumbs with the milk-egg mixture and quickly knead the shortcrust pastry. Depending on the quality of the flour, you may need a little more or a little less.
9. Roll the dough into a ball, wrap in cling film and put in the refrigerator for 30 minutes.
10. Divide the chilled dough into two unequal parts. Wrap the smaller part in film and return to the refrigerator. Roll out the remaining larger part of the dough into an even circle larger than the pie pan. Transfer the dough to the baking pan (Ø 24-25 cm) and distribute along the bottom and sides. Trim the edges of the dough with a knife. I put strips of parchment under the dough to easily remove the pie from the pan later.
11. Place the cherry filling on the dough and smooth it out.
12. Roll out the smaller part of the dough into a circle slightly larger than the diameter of the pan. Using a fluted knife, cut the dough into long strips about 1.5 cm thick. Preheat the oven to 180°C in advance.
13. Place the strips of dough on top of the filling in a lattice pattern. Those making this pie for the first time should better watch the video. It shows in detail how to do it.
14. For greasing, take the remaining egg white and a little sugar.
15. Whisk the egg white with a fork until light foam.
16. Brush the surface of the dough with the beaten egg white using a brush. Sprinkle sugar on top. Bake the pie in a preheated oven at 180°C on the top+bottom mode for about 40 minutes until golden brown. The exact time depends on your equipment. Let the finished cherry pie cool slightly in the pan, then remove it. Enjoy your meal!

title: MINCED MEAT WITH CHEESE, TASTY AND SIMPLE
instructions:
1. Start by preparing the necessary products. Choose minced meat according to your taste. It can be minced meat from a certain type of meat, for example only pork or beef, or you can take a mixed one. You can take ready-made minced meat or make it yourself by grinding the meat in a blender or passing it through a meat grinder.
2. Cut the bacon into small pieces. You can take raw smoked or cooked-smoked bacon, whichever you prefer.
3. Cut the cheese into small cubes or grate it. Any cheese will work for this dish - hard, semi-hard, soft, like mozzarella. The main thing is that it is tasty, high-quality, without milk fat substitutes.
4. In a bowl, combine minced meat, bacon, cheese, vegetable oil and spices. Thoroughly mix the minced meat until smooth. If you want a more homogeneous consistency, you can blend the minced meat, cheese and bacon in a stationary blender.
5. Form even round patties from the minced meat. I got 6 patties.
6. Heat a frying pan over medium heat. If desired, you can add a little oil, but the fat that renders from the minced meat and bacon will be enough. Fry the patties on both sides until golden brown. How long to fry minced meat with cheese in a pan? Fry the minced meat with cheese over medium heat for about 4-5 minutes per side. The exact time will depend on the features of your stove and the chosen pan.
7. You can serve the patties with any side dish or make delicious homemade burgers from them. I made hamburgers from the patties. Place 1-2 lettuce leaves on the bottom part of the burger buns. Place the beef patty on top. Drizzle the patty with ketchup and mustard. Distribute tomato slices and pickles on top. Cover with the other half of the bun. Serve the homemade hamburgers immediately. Enjoy your meal!
